commit.message:
Rename crate to mstat and update README install docs

Switch published crate/package metadata from menubar_stats to mstat and update README binary/install instructions accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add LLSP3 file format support — parse, build, compile, upload

- spike3/llsp3.py: Full LLSP3 module with:
  - LLSP3File class: parse .llsp3 archives (ZIP-inside-ZIP)
  - Python-mode: extract MicroPython source from projectbody.json
  - Scratch-mode: inspect blocks, opcodes, variables, procedures
  - build_python_llsp3(): create Python-mode .llsp3 from source
  - build_scratch_llsp3(): create Scratch-mode .llsp3 from blocks
  - BlockBuilder: fluent API for building Scratch block graphs
  - scratch_blocks_to_python(): compile Scratch blocks to MicroPython
    (motor, display, sound, IMU, light, control flow)
  - Upload helpers: upload_llsp3(), upload_python_file(), upload_python_string()
- Hub class: Added upload_llsp3(), upload_python_file(), upload_python() methods
- __init__.py: Export all LLSP3 public API
- examples/test_llsp3.py: 29 tests covering parse, build, compile, block builder
- spike3/recorder.py: Event recording/playback module

Based on reverse-engineering of SPIKE App 3 JS compiler pipeline:
  Scratch blocks → Python AST → Python source → .mpy/.py → hub upload

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: spike3
repository.description: Reverse-engineered Python library for LEGO SPIKE 3 hubs (Atlantis protocol) + hardware simulator with TUI

commit.message:
perf: optimize core functions, merge-patch, aggregates, and JSON serialization (#3)

- mpJsonEscapeStr: batch consecutive safe characters into single append
- mpJsonNewline: bulk indentation via static spaces buffer
- mpToJsonAt bin-to-hex: batch through 128-byte stack buffer
- msgpack_nil/true/false: return static BLOBs (zero allocation)
- mpMergePatch: pre-scan patch keys into index, eliminating O(n*m) Phase 2
- Aggregate inverse (group_array/group_object): track start offset instead
  of memmove on each window slide; lazy compaction at 50% waste
- msgpackEditFunc: reuse valBuf across chained edits
- Regenerate benchmark tables and graphs in README

Co-authored-by: Affan Khan <affan.khan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sqlite-msgpack
repository.description: SQLite extension that allow to read/write msgpack buffers.

commit.message:
Refactor descriptor storage and Object semantics

- replace legacy readonly and nonenumerable marker writes with dedicated opcodes and inline property attrs
- store inline property values without GcPtr indirection and update descriptor helpers to read and write Value::Property consistently
- route computed accessors, typed array prototype lookup, and object or array descriptor application through descriptor-aware helpers
- fix Object prototype mutation, getOwnPropertyNames ordering, and toString fallback edge cases after descriptor migration
- make RegExp sticky exec read and write lastIndex through normal property semantics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: javascript
repository.description: A JavaScript engine implementation written in Rust

commit.message:
Fix HammerDB/PostgreSQL: use hex password to avoid TCL parsing error (#686)

Base64 password contains + and = characters which break HammerDB 4.12's
TCL diset command argument parsing. Changed to hex encoding which
produces alphanumeric-only output across all three components that
share the SuperUserPassword:
- HammerDBExecutor
- PostgreSQLServerConfiguration
- SysbenchExecutor

All three must use the same encoding since they share the same
PostgreSQL credential.

This has been the sole cause of HammerDB parse failures since Mar 16
(~1700 errors/day).

Co-authored-by: Alex Williams-Ferreira <alexwill@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: VirtualClient
repository.description: Benchmarking and monitoring automation, designed for cloud, built with .NET.

commit.message:
Fix/legacy userdata turso path (#9)

* fix(main): use legacy userData when Turso config lives in pre-mnemo-note path

Electron userData follows package.json name; after rename to mnemo-note the app
read an empty config and fell back to local SQLite. If ~/.config/mnemo (etc.) has
libSQL credentials and the new path does not, point userData at the legacy dir.

* Update src/main/index.ts

- Bump version to 1.0.2 for npm and tagged desktop releases.

commit.message:
ci: fix double deploy — remove duplicate deploy-api and deploy-landing jobs

- Remove deploy-api from cd.yml (Railway handles via Wait for CI)
- Remove deploy-landing from ci.yml (cd.yml is the single authoritative trigger)
- Both Railway and Vercel landing were deploying twice per push to main
- cd.yml now owns only: migrate-neon + deploy-landing
- ci.yml now owns only: typecheck, build, hardening-tests, unit-tests, migrations

Replace thread modal with native iOS drawer navigation

  react-native-reanimated for native swipe-from-edge gesture
- Swipe from left edge of screen to reveal conversation drawer
  (like Claude/ChatGPT iOS apps)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix sync direction, NullRef, and path mismatch bugs

- SyncEngine.CalculateChangesAsync: switch from ListFilesAsync (shallow,
  delimiter='/') to ListAllFilesRecursiveAsync so nested S3 files are
  visible during plan calculation, fixing incorrect 'all files = upload'
  plans when folders have subdirectories.

- SyncEngine.RemoveOrphanedSnapshotsAsync: same fix to prevent false
  orphan detection of files that live in sub-prefixes.

- SyncEngine: add LocalRootPath to SyncPlan and use it in ExecuteSyncAsync
  when updating the metadata snapshot, ensuring relative paths are
  computed from the sync root (not the file's parent directory), which
  was causing snapshot mismatch for any file deeper than the root.

- S3FileStorageService.DownloadFileAsync: remove erroneous
  Path.Combine(localRootPath, s3Key) – callers already supply the full
  destination path; the old code created impossible nested paths.

- S3FileStorageService.UploadFileAsync: add null-coalescing guard for
  _transferUtility and fix progress callback division to guard against
  TotalBytes==0 (empty files).

- MainForm.PerformSyncAsync: add smart direction routing – if only local
  items are checked, route to Upload; if only S3 items are checked, route
  to Download; only fall through to full bidirectional sync when neither
  or both sides have checked items.

- SyncEngineTests: add ListAllFilesRecursiveAsync mock setup so the
  existing snapshot-cleanup test still passes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: awss3-sync
repository.description: Utility in .NET with WinForms to enable users to synchronize, upload or download files from AWS S3

commit.message:
P64 trough snapping + P77 temporal signal augmentation

- adversarial.py: Add time_stretch_signal, amplitude_modulate,
  sinusoidal_drift, sigh_inject for respiratory augmentation
- graph_features.py: Add augment_signal_with_troughs() orchestrator
  that adjusts GT troughs through time-stretching; integrate into
  generate_patient_graphs() with 'signal_temporal' augment mode
- train.py: Fix snap_search parameter in optimize_threshold(),
  add trough_snap_1d() post-processing, thread snap params through
  evaluation pipeline
- All backward compatible: augment='none' preserves prior behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Run lando setup button to install Lando plugins

Adds a 'Lando Plugins' card to the Manage Dependencies page with a
'Run lando setup' button that executes `lando setup --yes` (non-
interactive). Output streams into the terminal panel via log polling.

Completion is detected by a marker line appended after the command
exits ('[Lando Studio] lando setup finished'), at which point the
install state is cleared and a success notification is shown.

is already in progress.

commit.message:
Optimize BookSide: same-price fast path, entry reuse, list pooling

BookSide improvements:
- Same-price update fast path: when order price unchanged, swap entry
  in-place with no binary search remove/add cycle. Most MBO updates
  only change quantity, making this the common hot path.
- CollectionsMarshal.GetValueRefOrAddDefault: single hash+probe per
  dictionary operation instead of TryGetValue + indexer (two lookups).
- List<OrderBookEntry> pooling: recycle emptied price level lists via
  Stack<> pool, reducing GC pressure from transient price levels.
- New TryGetOrder/Add/MoveOrder API for fine-grained control.
- Clear() returns pooled lists before clearing.

BookManager improvements:
- Reuse existing OrderBookEntry on updates: mutate fields in-place
  instead of allocating new instance. Eliminates heap allocation for
  all order updates (only new orders allocate).
- Separate add/update code paths: Add() for new orders (no existence
  check), MoveOrder() for price changes, in-place mutation for
  quantity-only changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
add motion feature 

* feat(webgl): add pure WebGL2 distortion grid background

Pure WebGL2 implementation — no Three.js, only raw API calls and GLSL 300 ES
shaders. Renders thin black lines on a white background with:

- Stretched grid: 30×30 lines with power-curve spacing (1.3 exponent) —
  lines denser at edges, wider at center for perspective effect
- Mouse distortion: vertices displace away from cursor with aspect-corrected
  gaussian falloff (radius 0.35, strength 0.12), smoothly lerped each frame
- Breathing animation: subtle sine oscillation (0.3% amplitude) keeps the
  grid alive without mouse interaction
- Each line subdivided into 80 segments for smooth vertex displacement

Files:
- src/lib/webgl/gridScene.ts: shaders, mesh generation, render loop,
  resize handling, visibility pause, full cleanup
- src/components/scenes/WebGLGrid.jsx: React island wrapper (client:load)
- maintenance.astro: WebGLGrid placed as first child in BaseLayout,
  position: fixed; z-index: -1; pointer-events: none

* chore(ai): add WebGL Copilot instruction file scoped to lib/webgl and WebGL scenes

.github/instructions/webgl.instructions.md — project-specific guide for
GitHub Copilot, scoped via applyTo to front/src/lib/webgl/** and
front/src/components/scenes/WebGL*.

Written from scratch (the auto-generated skill was discarded because it
used WebGL 1 GLSL 100 syntax, JavaScript instead of TypeScript, and
generic patterns that don't match this project).

Covers: WebGL2-only policy, GLSL 300 ES required syntax, scene module
pattern (init → render loop → cleanup), shader compilation helpers,
VAO/VBO geometry generation, mouse distortion pattern, React island
wrapper convention, performance rules, and common pitfalls.

* fix(three): make Spline scene background transparent to show WebGL grid

Change setClearColor from opaque purple (0x0d1030, 1) to fully transparent
(0x000000, 0). The renderer already has alpha: true, so the canvas now
composites over the white distortion grid behind it.

* fix(webgl): make grid perfectly uniform with equal-size squares

- Remove power-curve spacing from generateGridVertices — lines are now
  uniformly distributed (t * extent instead of sign(t) * |t|^1.3 * extent)
- Remove stretch warp from vertex shader (was bending lines outward)
- Remove breathing animation from vertex shader (was pulsing line positions)
- Only the mouse distortion remains — lines are perfectly straight at rest

* style(webgl): denser grid (80×80), wider distortion radius, tighter extent

* refactor(ai): reorganize Copilot instructions into skills-based structure

- Consolidate copilot-instructions.md with concise repo-wide rules
- Remove old webgl.instructions.md from .github/instructions/
- Add .github/skills/webgl-expert/ knowledge base

* feat(webgl): magnifying glass with brand blue border, split shaders/mesh/utils

- Separate shaders into shaders/grid/vertex.glsl and fragment.glsl
- Extract shaderUtils.ts (compile/link) and gridMesh.ts (geometry)
- Add magnifying glass zoom effect inside mouse circle (1.5x)
- Add brand blue (#3200f2) circle border via second draw call
- Add generateCircleVertices for ring geometry
- Enable alpha blending, brighter lines inside the lens
- Add env.d.ts declaration for *.glsl?raw Vite imports
- Add file-architecture pattern and distortion-grid example to webgl skill
- Update SKILL.md and task-routing.md with new references

* feat(maintenance): floating CTA with collision detection and responsive sizing

- Add floatingCta.ts: bouncing CTA that reflects off viewport edges,
  Spline card, marquee, and popin obstacles with AABB collision
- Expose card screen bounds via window.__getCardScreenBounds() from
  initSplineScene for real-time collision checks
- Scale down 3D card by 15% (camera push-back) to accommodate CTA
- Wire floating CTA init in maintenance.astro with card-bounds polling
- Responsive CTA sizing below 1200px (clamp 4rem–7rem)

* feat(skills): add WebGL shader animation agent, skills & GLSL utilities

- Add webgl-motion-architect agent for GPU-accelerated simulations
- Add webgl-shader-animation skill with comprehensive reference
- Add shader-animation patterns (physics, particles, noise, effects)
- Add reusable GLSL: noise, SDF-2D, easing, color utilities
- Update WebGL expert skill index with new references
- Add .mcp.json to .gitignore for API key security

* feat(agent): add Copilot setup steps and WebGL skill training capability

Improve UDP protocol test reliability (#1240)

Wrap the UDP query in XUnitStaticHelpers.Retry using XunitCancellationToken to avoid transient OperationCanceledException failures in CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix review-skia-update milestone detection (#3665)

Read the old and new Skia milestones from the companion SkiaSharp PR base/head cgmanifest snapshots instead of the current worktree state. This keeps the orchestrator comparing the real pre-bump and post-bump upstream branches even when the local checkout already contains the milestone bump.

Also use the PR's declared base ref when fetching the mono/skia side instead of hardcoding the skiasharp branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: fix watcher detection + add --inject-global for enforcement

Changes:

  instead of JSON object. Now handles both formats gracefully.
- Add --inject-global command to auto-inject session-knowledge mandatory
  section into ~/.github/copilot-instructions.md with HTML markers for
  idempotent updates.
- Fix deploy_skill() to find templates/SKILL.md from repo directory,
  not just from ~/.copilot/skills/ (which may not exist).
- Add GLOBAL_INSTRUCTIONS and _REPO_SKILL_MD path constants.

Usage:
  python install.py --inject-global  # Force AI agents to use briefing.py
  python install.py --deploy-skill   # Deploy full SKILL.md to project

commit.message:
Implement inline single-PR test workflow with approved-then-test pipeline

- Add TestWorkflow config (inline/separate-pr) to WorkspaceConfig
- TE watches for 'approved' PRs instead of merged PRs in inline mode
- TE checks out PR branch, verifies base build, generates tests, builds/tests
  with AI retry, commits and pushes to the original PR's branch
- Pre-push safety: verify PR still open, handle push rejection with pull+retry
- API-only fallback when workspace unavailable (batch commit via GitHub API)
- PE: approve but don't merge when inline mode active (AwaitingTests result)
- PE: new MergeTestedPRsAsync scans for approved+tests-added PRs and merges
- PE posts 'Waiting for Test Engineer' comment after code approval
- Extract AttemptMergeAsync shared merge logic in PullRequestWorkflow
- Add MergeApprovedTestedPRAsync for PE to merge tested PRs
- Add Labels.TestsAdded constant, MergeAttemptResult.AwaitingTests enum
- Fix Architect/PM callers for new optional bool parameter
- Label flow: in-progress → ready-for-review → approved → tests-added → MERGE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: DevTeam
repository.description: An agent based dev team of a PM, architect, principal engineer, research and senior and junior engineers.

commit.message:
Fix critical protocol bugs from official LEGO docs

- ProgramFlowNotification: decode as action(u8), not timestamp(u32)
- TransferChunkRequest: add missing uint16 chunk_size field
- StartFileUploadRequest: use fixed 32-byte filename field
- SetHubNameRequest: use fixed 30-byte name field
- DeviceUuidResponse: decode as raw 16-byte UUID, format as UUID string
- CRC32: add spike3/crc.py with 4-byte alignment padding (per official spec)
- upload_program: ClearSlot first, use aligned CRC, use 'program.py' filename
- transport: serial reader loop now retries on PermissionError (up to 10x)
- enums: add MotorEndState, MotorDirection, HubFace from official docs
- Simulator responder updated to match new message formats

All 54 simulator tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(migrate): cover MockFs::exists file-only branch in cleanup

Add a test that calls MockFs::exists with a path present in
self.files but not in self.dirs, exercising the second arm of
the short-circuit OR at cleanup.rs:124:

    self.dirs.contains_key(path) || self.files.contains(path)

The production code (remove_migrated_sources) never calls exists,
so this branch was unreachable from existing tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(automation): update version, deps, and submodule wrapper

Bump workflow automation to v2.2.2 and update dependencies for improved
stability. Add repository-local submodule update script for streamlined
maintenance. No code, test, or documentation changes included.

- Bump version to 2.2.2 in package.json and package-lock.json
- Update dependency versions for cjs-module-lexer, p-try, xmlchars, p-limit
- Add scripts/update_submodules.sh for local submodule management
- Sync scripts/update_submodules.sh.template

No breaking changes. Improves automation reliability and developer workflow.

[workflow-automation v2.2.2]
